model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimizer and schedule are now much stronger, so the next likely bottleneck is model capacity. A modest width increase in both the conv trunk and MLP head should improve representation quality without materially complicating the script.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current wider model is working well, so the next low-complexity capacity increase is one extra pooled conv stage. That should improve feature hierarchy while keeping the classifier compact because the spatial map shrinks further.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, the model is still seeing well under one epoch of data. A moderate batch-size increase should expose it to substantially more examples without changing the training logic or optimizer geometry.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The fixed-step regime is responding strongly to larger batches, so the next efficient move is to process even more examples per step while nudging the learning rate upward to match the larger effective batch.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The fixed-step budget continues to favor seeing more unique examples, and the last batch-size increase was strongly beneficial. Pushing batch size further with a matched learning-rate increase is the most direct next test before attempting more structural changes.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The run is still benefiting disproportionately from processing more examples within the fixed 256-step horizon. Increasing batch size again, with a small matching learning-rate bump, is the clearest continuation of the current winning trend.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The training loop is now making substantial progress within the fixed budget, so the next likely limiter is representational capacity rather than optimization. A moderate width increase in both the feature extractor and head is a simple way to test that without changing the overall recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now substantially stronger and seeing enough data that mild regularization is worth testing. A small amount of label smoothing is a low-complexity way to improve generalization without disturbing the rest of the successful training recipe.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing moved validation loss in the wrong direction, so the next useful step is to remove it and return to the better plain cross-entropy objective before trying other changes.","secondary_modes":["micro","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the current large-batch, short-horizon regime, AdamW may benefit from a shorter second-moment timescale so it adapts faster early in training. Lowering beta2 is a compact way to test that without changing the rest of the winning setup.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The lower beta2 did not help, so restore the better AdamW moments and test a slightly lighter weight decay. With the current higher-capacity model and large-batch regime, regularization may now be a bit stronger than necessary.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 20, 1), 8)","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), 16)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With the current large-batch setup and already fast early learning, the existing warmup may be longer than necessary. A shorter warmup is a compact way to spend more of the fixed horizon at full learning rate without changing the rest of the recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), 16)","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 20, 1), 8)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The recent regularization and warmup edits both moved away from the best result, so the next useful candidate is to restore the stronger-known setting before using the remaining budget on a different axis.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current configuration is close to a strong basin, so a small upward learning-rate adjustment is a reasonable next probe. With the same warmup and cosine schedule, this tests whether the model is still slightly under-stepping early in the fixed budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Higher learning rate alone was worse, but the strongest gains earlier came from processing more examples per fixed step. Restore the better learning rate and test one more batch-size increase to continue that trend.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The clearest recurring signal in this run is that the fixed-step budget benefits from more examples per optimization step. A final upward batch-size move is the most direct continuation of the strongest trend seen so far.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The most reliable improvement signal throughout the run has been larger batches under the fixed 256-step horizon. With only a couple of trials left, the highest-upside move is to push that winning axis further.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 640","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The dominant signal across the run is that larger batches consistently improve validation loss under the fixed-step budget. For the final shot, the highest-upside continuation is one more increase along that same axis.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}
